introduction: We will be analyzing our help desk performance for the month of July 2025, with a focus on the nature of tickets received. This will help us identify common issues and improve our user support services.

subsections.heading: Overview of Help Desk Tickets
subsections.content:
• On average, our help desk receives 200 tickets daily
• 35% of tickets are related to connectivity issues
• 25% of tickets concern issues with the user interface
• Approximately 15% of tickets are about billing related queries
• About 10% of tickets are related to general user complaints or suggestions
• 5% of tickets cover miscellaneous issues not categorized under the above points
• The remaining 10% of tickets are escalated to higher-level support teams for resolution
subsections.justification: This content gives a broad overview of the types of tickets, but does not specifically address the percentage of tickets related to password resets.

subsections.heading: Breakdown of Password-Related Issues
subsections.content:
• Most password-related issues are due to forgetfulness or misplaced password details
• We've noticed a significant increase in password-related tickets during public holidays
• Approximately 30% of all password-related issues are resolved within 10 minutes
• Our team has implemented a self-service password reset tool to minimize such issues
• About 40% of users have successfully used the self-service tool for password resets
• We are actively working on improving our password security protocols
subsections.justification: While this slide discusses password-related issues, it does not provide the percentage of password reset tickets in July 2025.

subsections.heading: Opportunities for User Support Improvement
subsections.content:
• We are exploring automation options for common issues to reduce ticket volume
• User education programs could help reduce the number of tickets related to common issues
• We are considering extending our help desk hours to provide round-the-clock support
• A more user-friendly interface could reduce the number of tickets related to UI issues
• We are looking into ways to make our billing process more transparent to reduce billing-related tickets
• We are considering implementing a user feedback system to understand and address common user complaints
subsections.justification: This slide discusses potential improvements to user support, not the percentage of password reset tickets in July 2025.

conclusion: Our analysis shows a significant portion of help desk tickets are related to password resets. We need to explore solutions to reduce this number, such as self-service password reset options or enhanced user education.
